name: Benin
avoid: April to July and again in September-October bring the wettest southern roads, so Ouidah, Ganvié jetties, and market days in Cotonou get slower and muddier.
Northbound travel to Pendjari is long; if you only have a week, keep the wildlife leg tight and use the best available intercity coach instead of chaining multiple bush taxis.
Carry small XOF cash for zémidjans, boatmen, and heritage-site add-ons because cards are unreliable outside larger Cotonou hotels.
stopoverNote: Cotonou (COO) is the practical gateway. Most India itineraries work best as an open-and-close in Cotonou, then a clockwise overland loop through Ouidah, Ganvié, Abomey, and the Atacora north for Pendjari.

cities.name: Cotonou
cities.notes: Benin's transport hub for markets, beach evenings, Fondation Zinsou, and the easiest launch point for Ouidah and Ganvié.

cities.name: Ouidah
cities.notes: Vodun heritage, the Route des Esclaves, Temple of Pythons, beaches, and Benin's most important Atlantic memory trail.

cities.name: Abomey
cities.notes: The old Dahomey royal capital with the UNESCO palaces, court history, and an easy inland cultural stop on the way north.

cities.name: Ganvié
cities.notes: A stilt-village lagoon settlement near Abomey-Calavi best experienced by pirogue with a local boatman and guide.

cities.name: Pendjari National Park
cities.notes: Benin's flagship safari reserve for elephants, antelope, birdlife, and long dry-season game drives from Tanguiéta.

itinerary.sim: MTN Benin or Moov Africa Benin are the safest all-route picks; expect roughly ₹420–₹1,260 for a starter SIM/eSIM and enough data for maps, WhatsApp, and booking confirmations.

days.theme: Land in Benin with market energy, museum context, and a first coastal evening without straying far from the airport.

activities.name: Airport taxi or zémidjan-assisted taxi from Cotonou Cadjehoun Airport into Haie-Vive or Ganhi
Fondation Zinsou contemporary art visit
Dantokpa Market guided walk
Place de l'Étoile Rouge and Place des Martyrs drive-by circuit
Fidjrossè Beach sunset stop

days.theme: Use a second Cotonou day for history, crafts, and easy local hops before the heritage coast begins.

activities.name: Notre Dame de Miséricorde Cathedral stop
Centre Artisanal de Cotonou shopping loop
Route des Pêches beach-road outing by taxi or zémidjan
Marché Ganhi produce and spice stop
Short Gozem or zémidjan hops around Haie-Vive and Ganhi

days.theme: Follow Benin's most important heritage trail from town shrines to the Atlantic slave-route memorials.

activities.name: Intercity bus or bush taxi from Cotonou to Ouidah
Temple of Pythons entry
Ouidah Museum of History at the Portuguese Fort
Route des Esclaves walk plus Door of No Return memorial
Sacred Forest of Kpassè entry or local guide donation

days.theme: Slow down for beach time and museum depth if you want a fuller Ouidah read before turning inland.

activities.name: Plage de Ouidah morning beach walk
Guided Vodun history walk around central Ouidah temples and squares
Zémidjan hops between town center, museum quarter, and beach road
Return stop at Door of No Return for sunset photographs

days.theme: Trade roads for water and spend a full day understanding Benin's famous stilt village by pirogue.

activities.name: Bush taxi from Ouidah to Abomey-Calavi jetty via Cotonou
Ganvié shared motorized pirogue tour
Private boat upgrade with local guide
Canal-side market and stilt-house photo stops
Zémidjan hops around Abomey-Calavi jetties

days.theme: Move inland for Dahomey royal history, UNESCO palace courtyards, and a slower provincial evening.

activities.name: Intercity bus or bush taxi from Abomey-Calavi/Cotonou to Bohicon and onward to Abomey
Royal Palaces of Abomey UNESCO site and Historical Museum entry
Local palace guide inside the museum complex
Place Goho and royal quarter walk
Zémidjan hops around Abomey and Bohicon

days.theme: Use an extra Abomey day for museum depth, craft shopping, and a gentler pace before the long northern road.

activities.name: Artisan and textile shopping around Bohicon market
Second museum pass or special exhibition donation
Bush taxi scouting for the northbound coach to Djougou/Natitingou
Evening zémidjan ride through the royal quarter

days.theme: Commit to the long northern transfer, then use the late afternoon for your first Pendjari wildlife session.

activities.name: Intercity coach or bush taxi from Abomey/Bohicon to Natitingou or Tanguiéta
Shared taxi from Tanguiéta to Pendjari lodge area
Pendjari National Park foreign visitor entry
Mandatory guide and shared vehicle contribution for an afternoon drive
Sunset lookout around the Pendjari Safari Lodge plains

days.theme: Go out early for the best game-viewing light, then choose between more wildlife or an Atacora nature stop.

activities.name: Full morning game drive in Pendjari
Park guide supplement for birding or tracking focus
Tanougou Falls side trip from Tanguiéta area
Shared taxi back to Tanguiéta or Natitingou for onward planning

days.theme: Keep a spare Pendjari day only if you want one more safari loop before the long ride south.

activities.name: Second shared game drive or private vehicle top-up
Tanguiéta market supply stop before departure
Overland coach setup for the return to Cotonou

connections.method: Intercity buses, bush taxis, or a charter taxi along the RNIE1 coast road are the standard links west
The practical move is a bush taxi back toward Cotonou/Abomey-Calavi, then a pirogue from the Ganvié jetties
Return by boat to Abomey-Calavi, then continue inland by intercity bus or bush taxi via Bohicon
Long-haul coaches or chained bush taxis toward Natitingou/Tanguiéta are the normal northern route, followed by a lodge transfer
Return through Tanguiéta or Natitingou by coach; some travelers break the journey in Parakou, but the all-day bus south is the simplest budget finish

extras: November to March is the cleanest all-round window: southern heritage stops stay drier and Pendjari wildlife sightings improve as water sources shrink.
Zémidjans are fast and cheap in Cotonou, Ouidah, and Abomey, but agree the fare before you sit down and carry a light scarf or helmet liner.
Ganvié boat prices vary by whether you share or charter a pirogue, so ask your hotel or jetty guide to quote both before boarding.
Ouidah's memorial sites are emotionally heavy; many travelers like pairing them with a quiet beach sunset rather than rushing straight back to Cotonou.
Pendjari works best with at least one dawn drive; wildlife movement drops sharply once the afternoon heat sets in.
